title: 'Sinners' Wins Best Original Screenplay at 2026 Oscars
text: Ryan Coogler's film "Sinners" earned the Best Original Screenplay award at the 98th Academy Awards, marking a significant milestone in cinema history. With this victory, Coogler became only the second Black writer to win in this category, following Jordan Peele's 2018 triumph for "Get Out." Historic Achievement: Prior to the Oscar win, Coogler had already made history by becoming the first Black winner in the BAFTA Original Screenplay category for the same film. The screenplay defeated other notable contenders, including "Marty Supreme" and "Sentimental Value." Michael B. Jordan's Recognition: The film's star, Michael B. Jordan, received his first Oscar nomination for Best Actor in a Leading Role. In "Sinners," he portrays identical twins named Smoke and Stack, delivering a performance that has garnered significant industry attention. Competition and Recognition: Coogler triumphed against strong competition in the screenplay category. Industry observers and viewers have praised the film's tight narrative structure and ambitious storytelling, with some calling it "a modern classic." The win represents continued progress toward greater diversity and representation among major award-winning screenwriters in Hollywood.
